title: yyj-5: ToUnicode CMap fallback (conditional on yyj-1 findings)
description: If yyj-1 spike confirms mupdf's addFont attaches /ToUnicode automatically, this bead is closed unimplemented. If not, build the ToUnicode CMap from fontkit's cmap and inject it into the font dict via PDFObject manipulation. The CMap maps glyph IDs (CIDs in Identity-H) back to unicode code points so pdfjs.getTextContent / Ctrl+F / accessibility tools can extract source text from output PDFs. Without ToUnicode, emitted text is visually correct but unreadable to extraction — silent stop-ship bug.

title: yyj-4: rewrite emitText to use shaping + TJ glyph indices
description: Replace the current emitText '(text) Tj' emission with shaped TJ. For each run: (1) shape via yyj-2 helper, (2) emit BT … <font sizePt Tf> <x y Tm> [<glyphHex>(adjustment)<glyphHex>...] TJ … ET. Glyph IDs serialized as 4-hex 16-bit values (Identity-H). GPOS xAdvance adjustments emitted as inline numeric elements in the TJ array, in -1000ths of font units (PDF spec convention: positive = move pen left = tighter; we want negative for tighter spacing in default GPOS sign convention — verify direction in unit test). yOffset/xOffset handled via per-glyph Tm if non-zero. Backward-compatible: callers still call emitText(el, ctx, registry); registry now exposes the fontkit Font instances per fontKey.
acceptance_criteria: Updated graftCs.ts emitText. graftCs.test.ts tests cover: (a) ligature emission — emitText for a <text> with content 'office' produces a TJ array containing the 'ffi' ligature glyph hex; (b) kerning emission — emitText for 'Ta' produces a TJ array with at least one numeric inline adjustment between glyph hex elements; (c) Tm positioning matches the SVG x/y; (d) tspan inheritance still honoured (font-family, font-style, font-weight cascade). Existing emitText tests rewritten to expect TJ shape, not Tj.

title: Graft engine 'deletes' source elements via mask overlay, not content-stream rewrite
description:
The graft engine handles deletion of source elements (e.g. user
imports a PDF, deletes a text element, exports) by overlaying a
white-fill mask rectangle on top of the grafted source page. This
visually hides the deleted content but does NOT remove it from the
PDF content stream.

CONSEQUENCES:
- pdfjs getTextContent() and any text-extraction tool still finds
  the "deleted" text in the output.
- Ctrl+F in any PDF viewer finds the deleted text.
- Copy-paste from the exported PDF copies the deleted text.
- Screen readers / accessibility tools speak the deleted text.
- Search-engine indexing ingests the deleted text.

This is NOT a real delete; it's a visual cover. Fundamentally wrong
for a PDF editor.

DETECTED:
The conservative routing gate in fileio.ts gates deletions to
pdf-lib precisely because of this. Attempting to relax the gate
(working tree, 2026-04-26) failed golden gate 06 because pdfjs
extracts the still-present text from the graft-engine output. Gate
working as intended — caught a real fidelity regression.

SCOPE:
Affects every "delete a source element" workflow once the routing
gate is relaxed. Currently masked by the conservative routing.
design:
PROPER ARCHITECTURAL FIX: rewrite the grafted source's content stream
to omit the operators corresponding to deleted elements, instead of
clone-then-overlay-mask.

Required pieces:

1. Element ↔ content-stream-position correspondence layer.
   At MuPDF import time we get an SVG with one element per text run /
   path / image. Need to also capture which range of operators in the
   source page's content stream produced each emitted SVG element.
   Options:
   a) MuPDF device-trace API — instrument a custom device that records
      operator offsets while rendering. Most precise; unclear if mupdf-js
      exposes this.
   b) MuPDF structured-text output with positional info — coarser, may
      lose path-op granularity.
   c) Re-parse the page content stream client-side and match by
      coordinates from the imported SVG. Brittle but pure-JS.
   The correspondence map gets stored alongside the SourcePdfStore
   primary entry.

2. Content-stream rewriter in graftMupdf / graftCs.
   Replace the current 'graft page intact + append overlay stream'
   pattern. New pattern:
   - Graft page (preserves resources / fonts / images).
   - Compute set of deleted-element operator ranges from snapshot
     registry + correspondence map.
   - Stream-edit the grafted page's content stream: remove BT/ET text
     blocks (or q/Q-bracketed groups) at those ranges.
   - Append overlay stream for any modified / new elements (existing path).

3. Fail-closed routing.
   If correspondence can't be established for any deleted element
   (correspondence map missing / coordinate match ambiguous), refuse
   to take the graft path for that document — route to pdf-lib.
   Correctness over fidelity.

4. Update graft mask-rect emitter.
   The PdfRect mask primitive (graftCs.emitMaskRectOp) becomes
   reserved for genuine occlusion needs (e.g. modified elements
   where re-render-on-top covers the source). For deletions, the
   correct emitter is content-stream removal, not paint-over.

NON-NEGOTIABLE:
- No band-aids. Mask overlays for deletion are forbidden — they
  produce searchable "deleted" content.
- Don't relax shouldUseGraftEngine for deletions until this lands.

OPEN QUESTIONS:
- Does mupdf-js expose enough of the operator-trace API to build the
  correspondence map at import time?
- Path operators (m/l/c/h, q/Q stack) often share content-stream
  blocks across multiple SVG elements that came from one MuPDF
  flatten step. May need to split at finer granularity than BT/ET.
acceptance_criteria:
- Golden gate 06 (PDF delete-text) passes with the graft engine
  active (i.e. shouldUseGraftEngine returns true for deletions-only
  mixed layers).
- pdfjs getTextContent() on the exported PDF returns ZERO
  occurrences of any deleted text element's content.
- Copy-paste from the exported PDF (verified via headed Chromium
  dogfood) does not yield deleted text.
- All 10 existing gate stories remain green.
- A new gate story explicitly verifies text-extraction-after-delete
  (not just visual mask).
- Routing change in fileio.ts (allow deletions-only mixed layers
  through graft) re-applied as part of this work, with green gates.
notes:
WIP commit on main 2026-04-26 contains the failed routing-relax
attempt (graftBbox.ts tspan-aware bbox + fileio.ts routing relax).
Routing relax must be REVERTED before this bead can ship its own
solution — leaving it in place ships the broken state.

The graftBbox.ts tspan-aware textBox is independently valuable and
stays. sourceSnapshot.ts uses elementBboxMm at snapshot time, so
import-time bboxes are now more accurate; useful regardless of
whether mask emission ever fires.

Predecessor: vectorfeld-d3o (shipped the mask-emission band-aid).
This bead supersedes that approach for deletions.

title: [graft/phase 3] fontkit shaping for new (non-source) text
description: When the user creates new text via the text tool (AddElementCommand, not from PDF import), shape via fontkit.layout(string) using our bundled Carlito/Liberation Serif. Emit raw PDF TJ with glyph indices + positioning adjustments rather than pdf-lib drawText (which handles basic cases but not all OpenType features). Result: correct fi/fl ligatures in Carlito, correct GPOS kerning pairs for pairs like Ta/AV/Wa. ~200 LOC + tests for ligature presence in output TJ array.
design:
GOAL — Professional-grade text emission in the graft engine. Full
OpenType functionality: GSUB substitutions (ligatures fi/fl/ffl,
contextual alternates, small caps, fractions, …), GPOS kerning pairs
(Ta/AV/Wa/etc.), all features fontkit.layout exposes. No band-aids,
no kerning-only fallback.

WHY — Today's emitText writes `(text) Tj` with simple-encoded
WinAnsi-Latin font (addSimpleFont path). Two consequences:
  1. No GSUB → "office" stays as o-f-f-i-c-e instead of o-ﬃ-c-e.
  2. No GPOS → "Ta/AV/Wa" sit at default advance widths, looking
     loose to anyone with a calibrated eye.
Routing currently gates additions/modifications to pdf-lib because
of these fidelity gaps.

ARCHITECTURE — Switch text emission from simple-encoded Tj to
Type-0/CID-keyed TJ with explicit glyph indices and inline GPOS
positioning adjustments.

  Embed: outDoc.addFont(carlitoFont) — produces a Type-0 dict with
    Identity-H encoding (verified by yyj-1 spike).
  Shape: fontkit.layout(string, features) → GlyphRun with glyph IDs
    and per-glyph xAdvance/yAdvance/xOffset/yOffset.
  Emit: For each shaped run,
     /F1 12 Tf
     x y Tm
     [<00FA00120023 (-32) 0045>] TJ
    where each 2-byte hex group is a glyph CID, and each numeric
    inline element is an xAdvance adjustment in negative thousandths
    of font units (PDF spec convention).
  ToUnicode: outDoc.addFont auto-attaches a /ToUnicode CMap that maps
    glyph IDs back to source code points (verified by yyj-1 spike).
    If it doesn't, yyj-5 builds it from the fontkit cmap.

ROUTING — Relaxes shouldUseGraftEngine to allow mixed-with-additions
(yyj-6). Modifications also become safe through graft after yyj
because Carlito-via-graft is now visually equivalent to Carlito-via-
pdf-lib, and graft preserves all OTHER source content byte-for-byte.
Source-text modifications still want eb0 (use the source font, not
Carlito) but yyj closes the substitution-fidelity gap on its own.

PLAN B (rejected) — Stay simple-encoded, emit TJ with kerning-only
adjustments between Tj operators. Captures GPOS but not GSUB. Banned
by user decree: no kerning-only band-aid.

OPEN QUESTIONS resolved by yyj-1 spike:
  - Does addFont(font) produce a Type-0 CID font with Identity-H?
  - Does addFont attach a ToUnicode CMap automatically?
  - Round-trip: emit TJ via glyph IDs → re-open via mupdf and
    pdfjs → does extracted text match the source string?
  - If addFont produces a SimpleFont instead (against expectation),
    fall to plan A' — hand-roll the CID dict via PDFObject API.

SCOPE — IN: kerning, ligatures, contextual alternates, all standard
OpenType features for Latin/Greek/Cyrillic Carlito text. Subsetting
deferred to yyj-8 (optimization, not correctness). CJK/RTL deferred
(no fixture demands it).

NON-NEGOTIABLE: text extracted from output PDFs must equal source
strings (pdfjs.getTextContent contract). Verified at every emission
test + at the gate level. Loss of extractability is a stop-ship bug.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Output PDFs from the graft engine emit text via Type-0 CID fonts
   with Identity-H encoding, glyph-index TJ, and proper GPOS/GSUB.
2. Ligatures present in fontkit.layout output appear as ligature
   glyphs in the TJ array (assert by glyph ID against fontkit's
   'liga' substitution).
3. Kerning pairs (Ta/AV/Wa/We/etc.) carry GPOS adjustments inline
   in TJ (assert numeric elements present and non-zero).
4. pdfjs.getTextContent on a graft-emitted PDF returns the original
   source string verbatim (ToUnicode round-trip).
5. mupdf.toStructuredText.asText same.
6. Routing accepts mixed-with-additions (and ideally mixed-with-
   modifications) through graft.
7. All existing golden gates green; new ligature/kerning gate green.
8. dogfood:composite + dogfood:atrium green.

SHIP-BLOCKING: failing #4 or #5 (text becomes unextractable) is a
stop-ship bug. Output is unusable.
notes:
The user explicitly rejected the kerning-only Plan B. Full OpenType.
"professional grade", no band-aids, no quick fixes. (2026-04-26
session.)

Foundational for vectorfeld-eb0 (source-font reuse for in-place
modifications). yyj closes the gap for Carlito; eb0 closes it for
the source PDF's own embedded fonts.
close_reason: Shipped 2026-04-27. All 9 sub-beads closed (yio/of4/7t7/33a/giz-as-unneeded/87h/ufg/ahx, with clw deferred as P3 optimization). Acceptance: Type-0 Identity-H emission with GSUB ligatures + GPOS kerning; pdfjs.getTextContent round-trip verified at unit + gate level (gates 10/11). Plan B (kerning-only) rejected as required. See docs/worklog/2026-04-27-yyj-shipped.md.

title: [graft/spike 3/3] Read + tokenize content-stream operators via mupdf.js
description: Write scripts/spike/03-parse-opstream.ts. For page 0 of the noheader flyer, walk to its /Contents stream via PDFObject API (doc.getTrailer → Root → Pages → Kids[0] → Contents). Call readRawStream() to get raw bytes. Decode if /Filter is set (FlateDecode via node:zlib). Tokenize operators enough to locate a 'Tj' op whose string operand contains 'Kurzfristige'. Report: (1) is there a ready-made operator iterator on mupdf.js or do we roll our own? (2) what's the decoded stream shape (line-oriented? compact?). Determines blast radius of the in-place-edit path. Write findings to temp/spike-03-findings.md.
close_reason: PASS. Custom tokenizer 120 LOC (table-driven per PDF spec §7.8.2) parsed 16KB source content stream into 4264 tokens in 13ms. Op histogram: 106 TJ (array form, per-char positioning), 106 BT/ET text blocks, 112 q/Q save/restore, 27 BDC (tagged content markers — preserved through graft). Zero Tj (plain); source uses TJ exclusively. Finding specific TEXT requires ToUnicode CMap decoding (CID Identity-H fonts use 2-byte glyph indices, not WinAnsi chars) — tractable, +~100 LOC. Phase 3 total: ~250 LOC for tokenizer + CMap + rewrite. tempspike-03-findings.md + scripts/spike/03-parse-opstream.mjs captured.

title: Real-PDF export still has text kerning / per-char positioning issues
description: After vectorfeld-dns (leaf transform + tspan position fixes) the composite-via-playwright export is visually mostly correct — headline at top, body in middle, QR + flag at bottom. BUT user reports 'still messed up but better' on 2026-04-20. Residual: per-character spacing/kerning is wrong. Words run together (e.g. 'Ich entlastedurch mein Lektoral'). Root cause hypothesis: MuPDF emits multi-character runs in tspans with PER-CHARACTER x-arrays like x='100 108 116 124' for spacing control. Our drawText currently honours only the FIRST x value via tspan.getAttribute('x').split(/\s+/)[0], then lets pdf-lib lay out the rest with default Helvetica metrics (which don't match the source font's metrics). Fix: iterate per character in tspan when x has multiple values; for each char, drawText at its own (x, y). Or use pdf-lib's drawText with character-spacing if API supports. Verify visually with composite-via-playwright + headed Chromium screenshot.
close_reason: drawText now iterates per character when tspan x/y is a space-separated list. Real-flyer dogfood via headed Chromium: all body text reads cleanly (entlaste durch, Lektorat bei, Erfahrung all now correctly spaced). 3 new tests, 509→512 green. Commit 1d6dbf9.

title: Real-PDF export bugs: leaf-element transforms + <tspan> positioning
description: vectorfeld-9s9 shipped synthetic-test-only and missed two structural bugs that bite every real PDF import: (1) walk() applied transforms on container <g> elements but ignored them on leaf elements (text/path/rect/...), so MuPDF's flatten step (which puts transform=scale(pt-to-mm) directly on each leaf) was silently dropped — content rendered ~3x too large at wrong positions. (2) drawText only read x/y from the <text> element itself, ignoring positions on <tspan> children — but MuPDF emits text positioning ON THE TSPAN. Result: every imported text collapsed to (0,0) and ended up at the bottom-left of the exported PDF. Fixes: walk() now applies the element's own transform before drawing; drawText iterates tspans, drawing each at its own position. 4 new failing tests in test/roundtrip/svgToPdfRoundtrip.test.ts cover both regressions. Verified visually with composite-via-playwright: composite.pdf now matches the canvas layout. Lesson added to docs/lessons.md.
close_reason: Both bugs fixed: walk() now applies leaf transforms; drawText iterates tspans. 4 new failing-then-green tests in svgToPdfRoundtrip.test.ts. Real-PDF composite-via-playwright now produces a visually-correct composite.pdf (verified in headed Chrome PDF viewer). 509/509 tests green.

title: pdf-lib export crashes on non-WinAnsi characters (e.g. ◊ U+25CA)
description: Discovered during composite-via-playwright end-to-end test 2026-04-20. pdf-lib's StandardFonts.Helvetica uses WinAnsi encoding which cannot represent characters outside Latin-1 + extras. The noheader flyer contains '◊' bullets, causing pdf.save() to throw 'WinAnsi cannot encode "◊" (0x25ca)' — entire Export PDF fails, no file produced. Quick fix: substitute or drop unencodeable chars per element with a console.warn so the rest of the export succeeds. Proper fix (separate bead): embed a Unicode TTF font (DejaVu Sans, Noto, etc.) so we cover all common chars without substitution loss.
acceptance_criteria: (1) Synthetic SVG with non-WinAnsi chars (◊, em-dash, etc.) exports without throwing. (2) Substituted chars logged to console.warn. (3) Export PDF round-trip test still green. (4) Composite-via-playwright produces composite.pdf in temp/.
close_reason: safeEncode wrapper in pdfExport.ts catches WinAnsi encoding failures, drops unencodable chars per char with structured console.warn (codepoint listed). Composite-via-playwright now succeeds: 505/505 tests green incl. 2 new encoding tests; composite.pdf delivered (260KB, 1 page, A4); 48 text items extractable via pdfjs-dist getTextContent including German Üöä; only 8 ◊ bullets dropped from the noheader flyer (filed vectorfeld-jc6 for Unicode TTF embedding to preserve those).

title: PDF export via svg2pdf.js does not preserve imported-PDF fonts (round-trip breaks body text)
description: End-to-end test 2026-04-20: File > Open PDF > noheader.pdf, File > Open PDF as Background Layer > yellow BG.pdf, File > Export PDF produces composite.pdf (~1.2 MB, 1 page, valid PDF). But body text is visibly garbled — glyphs misaligned, characters missing (e.g. 'Kurzfristige Hilfe' renders as 'xzfristlge Hilfe'; 'Lektorat von Vortrag' becomes 'ktorlas Vertrragss'). Root cause: svg2pdf.js converts <text>/<tspan> to PDF native text using its own bundled font metrics (Helvetica/Times fallback), which don't match MuPDF's glyph positioning from the original (LiberationSerif etc.). The PDF thus has correct x/y coordinates but wrong glyph widths. SVG export of the same composite is pixel-perfect because it preserves the <text> elements verbatim; the browser renders them correctly because the browser has the real fonts. For round-trip fidelity we need one of: (a) embed original PDF fonts in the exported PDF (requires font subset extraction from source PDF during import), (b) outline all text on export (lossy, non-editable), (c) swap svg2pdf.js for a different engine that handles font embedding. See also vectorfeld-cd2 (import side) — the two together are the PDF-edit pivot's most critical open issue.
close_reason: Production File > Export PDF now uses pdf-lib engine (src/model/pdfExport.ts) end-to-end. Replaces svg2pdf.js + jsPDF for export. Text content survives PDF round-trip; <g transform> composes through walker matrix; primitives (text, path, rect, line, ellipse, circle, image) all supported. 491/491 tests green incl. 13 new round-trip integration tests with extractPdfTextItems position-aware assertions. Bundle: jspdf still imported by test fixture generators; svg2pdf.js fully unwired. Follow-up beads for: bundle cleanup, font-family preservation beyond Helvetica, rotation/skew on rect/ellipse becoming paths.

title: graftBbox.textBox accuracy for MuPDF-imported tspan-wrapped text
description:
graftBbox.textBox computes a conservative bbox for SVG text elements
using element-level x/y + font-size + char-count heuristics. For
text imported via MuPDF this is wrong:

- MuPDF emits each text run as <text><tspan x="..." y="..." font-size="...">…</tspan></text>.
  The TSPAN carries the actual baseline y and font-size; the parent
  <text> element's y/font-size attributes are often defaults or
  inherited.
- textBox uses elY (the parent's y) for the bbox top and elFontSize
  for height. For tspan-wrapped imports it picks up the wrong values.
- Width comes from char-count × fontSize × 0.55 — fine for plain
  text but ignores tspan x-arrays which give exact glyph positions.

Symptom: the d3o deletion-mask path emits white-fill rects in the
WRONG place when the deleted source element is MuPDF-imported text.
Verified against gate story 06: deleting the flyer headline emits a
mask near the BOTTOM of the page instead of over the headline.

Until this lands, fileio.shouldUseGraftEngine stays strict on the
deletion case (gates story 06 to pdf-lib). The d3o engine-side mask
emission is wired and unit-tested for synthetic shape cases, so
flipping the gate is a one-line change once textBox is rebuilt.

Fix sketch:
  - When tspans exist: take baseline y from the first tspan with a
    y attribute (fall back to elY).
  - Take font-size from each tspan's font-size attr (fall back to
    elFontSize).
  - Compute width per-tspan: when xs.length >= chars, use max(xs) -
    first-tspan-min(xs); when shorter, use the existing
    char-advance fallback.
  - Compute height: pick max font-size across tspans × 1.3.

Tests:
  - synthetic <text><tspan x="20" y="50" font-size="6">Hi</tspan>
    bbox top should be ~44, not elY-elFontSize.
  - tspan with x-array: width matches max-x − min-x exactly.
  - dogfood gate (story 06 with relaxed gate): mask covers the
    headline, not random bottom region.

~80 LOC + tests in graftBbox.ts. Once shipped, relax fileio gate to
allow mixed-with-deletions-only and re-record story 06 master to
see "headline removed" in the rendered PDF.
close_reason: Done. graftBbox.textBox is tspan-aware (parses tspan x/y/font-size, falls back to element attrs, unions across tspans) and 20/20 graftBbox tests green including all 6 vectorfeld-38q regressions. Original mask-placement motivation became moot when vectorfeld-enf switched deletion to mupdf.applyRedactions (excises source content stream instead of overlaying white-fill rects). Story 06 gates green via graft post-yyj. All 827 unit tests + 11/11 gates green.

title: Graft engine: track source-element deletions for the mixed path
description:
The graft engine's mixed path masks + re-renders MODIFIED source
elements, but it doesn't yet handle DELETIONS. A user who imports a
PDF, deletes a text element, and exports gets the original text back —
the engine byte-copies the source page intact and the deleted
element's snapshot can't be iterated (WeakMap, no .size).

This is the bug that surfaced when wiring u7r against gate story 06:
the conservative gate now skips graft entirely whenever
currentSourceCount(layer) !== expectedSourceCount(layer), routing
deletions back to pdf-lib. Correctness preserved, fidelity lost.

To fix: extend the snapshot system to track REMOVED source elements
with their bbox at deletion time, then have the graft engine emit a
mask rect for each removed element's bbox (same primitive used for
modified elements).

Implementation sketch:

  - At snapshot time, build a per-layer Map<UID, {weakRef, bboxMm}>.
    UID stamped on the element via data-vf-snap-uid; bbox captured via
    elementBboxMm.
  - At export time, walk the registry. For each entry whose weakRef
    has gone (element removed from DOM), emit emitMaskRectOp(bbox).
  - Update graftClassify to surface a removedElementBboxes list on
    'mixed' classifications so graftExport can render them.
  - Once shipped, relax shouldUseGraftEngine in fileio.ts to accept
    deletions (drop the count-equality check).

Tests:
  - mixed layer with one deleted text → mask covers that text's bbox.
  - mixed layer with deletion + modification → both masks emitted.
  - foundation graft + non-foundation overlay with deletions in either:
    correct mask placement.

~120 LOC + tests. Once this lands, gate story 06 can flip to graft
output (re-record master) and the master diff visually shows the
deleted text is gone.
close_reason: Shipped engine-side deletion tracking (sourceSnapshot registry + classifyLayer.removedBboxes + graftExport mask emission). Synthetic tests verify mask emission. Production gate stays strict pending textBox rebuild — visually verified that imported-text bbox is mis-located for MuPDF tspan-wrapped text. Filed follow-up. 769 unit tests + 10/10 gates green.

title: Graft engine: single-page-stacking when layers share artboard
description:
exportViaGraft currently emits ONE PAGE PER LAYER (MVP scope chosen in
hnj). For the canonical casual-PDF use case — a single-page imported PDF
+ zero or one overlay layer that should land on the same page — this
matches. For the compositing flow (foreground + background PDF, both on
one artboard), it emits 2 pages, which is a functional regression vs.
the current pdf-lib pipeline.

Before wiring exportViaGraft into fileio.ts (vectorfeld-u7r), the engine
needs to support single-page-stacking:

- Group layers by their target output page (driven by source-page index
  for graft/mixed; overlay-only layers stack onto the same page as the
  preceding graft/mixed layer, or onto a fresh page if no graft layer
  precedes them).
- For each group: graft the foundation page (first graft/mixed layer's
  source page), then append overlay content streams from the remaining
  graft/mixed/overlay layers.
- Determine pageHeightPt from the foundation page's MediaBox; subsequent
  overlays use that same height.
- Mask + re-render only applies to the foundation graft layer's modified
  elements (other graft layers in the group already grafted on top).

Tests:
- 2 graft layers → 1 page; both source contents visible.
- 1 graft + 1 overlay → 1 page; overlay drawn over graft.
- 2 overlay-only → 1 page (artboard size); both layers' content present.
- multi-page source (rare in casual flow) → opt-in via opts; default
  behaviour stays single-page.

~150 LOC + tests in src/model/graftExport.ts.
close_reason: Refactored exportViaGraft to single-page-stacking. Foundation = first graft/mixed in DOM order; non-foundation layers render via overlay (lossy for non-foundation graft, acceptable MVP). MediaBox follows foundation source PDF when grafting. Net 7 LOC down despite new logic. 750 unit tests green. Unblocks u7r.

Saves via outDoc.saveToBuffer({compress:true}). Determinism pinned (no timestamp leakage). New file src/model/graftExport.ts (~100 LOC) + integration tests using the dogfood fixtures.

title: [graft/phase 3] In-place source text edit via grafted source font
description: The pickiest use case: user clicks a source-PDF text element, edits the string. Today would become mask+re-render in our Carlito — visible seam. Correct version: extract the source font bytes from the grafted PDF (FontFile2 stream), hand to fontkit, re-shape the edited string with the SOURCE font. Emit TJ rewriting just that op-range in the source content stream. Result: edited text blends invisibly with untouched surroundings — same font, same kerning style, same ligature behavior. Requires spike 3 content-stream parser + spike 2 content-stream writer. ~300 LOC.
close_reason: Shipped 2026-04-27. All 5 sub-beads closed (wvy/wgv/ryn/m11/kkj). Modifications on source-PDF text now re-emit in the source's original embedded font; Carlito fallback for coverage gaps. Gate 10 carries the Calibri-* defense assertion.

For untouched regions this should be IDENTICAL after graft (modulo PDF xref offsets). Add as a regression gate for the graft engine. If bytes diverge, the test should print which operator ranges differ so future regressions are easy to track down. Also: headed-Chromium screenshot diff via pixelmatch as a cross-check.

title: [graft/phase 2] New engine: src/model/graftExport.ts
description: New export engine. Entry: exportViaGraft(doc: DocumentModel, sourcePdfHandles: Map<layerId, mupdfHandle>) → Uint8Array. Strategy: (1) Create new mupdf PDFDocument. (2) For each layer in z-order: if layer has a sourcePdfHandle AND no element in it has touchesSource=true → graftPage that layer's page into the new doc. If layer is overlay-only (SVG-authored or edits) → render via existing pdf-lib drawing path (now only used for new content). (3) For each source-touching element: Phase-2 compromise = mask with white rectangle + re-render the edited element on top via pdf-lib (preserves everything around it; the edited element uses our font stack). Phase 3 upgrades this to content-stream rewrite. (4) Save. ~400 LOC.
close_reason: Engine landed via hnj (commit 1cd7f71). All sub-beads (wjj-1..9: tsi, fx8, 68p, 2oi, am6, uuz, e1j, ne4, hnj) closed and composed into src/model/graftExport.ts. wjj was the meta tracking item; engine works end-to-end and is verified by 8 integration tests. Next: u7r wires it into fileio.ts exportPdf.

Used by export path to decide: graft-through vs mask+re-render vs content-stream-rewrite. No behavior change yet — this bead just adds the metadata. ~40 LOC + 3 test cases.

title: [graft/phase 2] Tag SVG elements with source-PDF back-references

The op-offset is the bridge for in-place text edits later. Requires spike 3 findings to know whether we get offsets from mupdf.js or need a custom tokenizer. ~50 LOC + dependent on spike-3.

title: Embed Unicode TTF font in pdf-lib export to preserve non-WinAnsi chars
description: Per vectorfeld-ape, the pdf-lib export currently uses StandardFonts.Helvetica which has WinAnsi encoding. Characters outside Latin-1 + extras (e.g. ◊ U+25CA used as bullets in Word PDFs, em-dashes, fancy quotes, CJK, math symbols) get DROPPED during export with a console.warn. Fix: bundle a permissively-licensed Unicode TTF (DejaVu Sans / Noto Sans) and pdf.embedFont(ttfBytes, { subset: true }) so the export preserves arbitrary text. Bundle hit ~80-200KB (subset to chars actually used). Acceptance: composite-via-playwright produces composite.pdf with ◊ bullets visible.
close_reason: Embedded Carlito (Calibri clone) + Liberation Serif (Playfair Display substitute) via @pdf-lib/fontkit. Non-WinAnsi chars like ◊ now survive Export PDF. Real-flyer composite visually matches source fonts at the glyph level. Commit a8d2fe6.

title: PDF export: yellow-BG composite has left-edge white margin + possible content clipping
description: Observed in composite.pdf (output of end-to-end test). The yellow background rectangle appears truncated on the left — a ~15-20mm white strip is visible at the left edge, and some content near the page edges may be clipped. SVG export of the same composite renders correctly (yellow fills full page). Likely cause: svg2pdf.js handles viewBox/transform composition differently than browser, or clipPath elements (112 clipPaths in this PDF's defs) don't translate cleanly. Investigate: (1) does svg2pdf.js honor the inherited scale transform from flattenAndScalePdfLayer, (2) does it process <defs> clipPath correctly, (3) is the issue specific to this PDF or reproducible with simpler content. Lower priority than font fidelity but part of the same round-trip-fidelity-via-svg2pdf.js story.
notes: 2026-05-04 cut-off mid-investigation. WIP debug instrumentation in df591f5 (drawImage geometry log + embed-fail warn). title: Multi-document UI + cross-document clipboard (DocumentState Phase 2)
description: The defining feature of the PDF-edit pivot. Phase 1 of the DocumentState refactor landed; this builds the UI on top of it. Concrete sketch: (1) EditorProvider holds a mutable list { id, model: DocumentModel, history: CommandHistory, state: DocumentState }[] instead of one of each. (2) A tab strip UI above the canvas (or under the menu bar) lists open docs with close-buttons. Clicking a tab calls setActiveDocument(state) and swaps docRef.current, historyRef.current accordingly. (3) File -> Open PDF creates a new document entry and switches to it (instead of replacing the current doc). (4) Clipboard ref lifted from EditorProvider to a module-level or app-level location so it's shared across all open docs; copySelection/pasteClipboard read from the shared clipboard and operate on the active doc's model/history. (5) Keyboard shortcuts Ctrl+Tab, Ctrl+1..9 — nice-to-have.

title: File > Open PDF as Background Layer (compositing primitive)
description: Single-doc model + replace-on-open makes the composite-PDF use case require: open foreground, Ctrl+A, Ctrl+C, open background (destroys canvas), Ctrl+V, Ctrl+Shift+[. Verified working end-to-end today with temp/Flyer*.pdf. Users would not discover this workflow. Proposal: add second File menu item 'Open PDF as Background Layer…' that (1) creates a new layer named after filename, (2) imports the PDF into it, (3) sends the new layer to bottom of layer stack, (4) does not touch existing layers. Covers 80% of compositing without waiting for vectorfeld-4w7 multi-doc tabs.
close_reason: Added File > Open PDF as Background Layer... menu item. Imports a PDF as a new <g data-layer-name> positioned BEFORE existing layers (bottom of z-stack = rendered first = behind). Layer name = filename minus '.pdf' extension, truncated to 40 chars with ellipsis; empty/whitespace stems fall back to 'Background'. Reuses flattenAndScalePdfLayer so new layer has direct children (not wrappers). Does not touch viewBox or clear existing layers; defs are appended. Bug found and fixed during verification: clearSelection() was being called BEFORE the layer insertion, so LayersPanel's selection subscription saw the old state; moved to end of function. Live-verified against temp/Flyer*.pdf — 3-click composite (Open PDF noheader.pdf → Open PDF as Background Layer yellow BG.pdf → done). Both layers show in Layers panel with correct names and z-order. 8 new unit tests: insertion position, existing-layer preservation, viewBox not touched, pt→mm scale applied, filename sanitization (strip extension, truncate, Background fallback). 461 tests green.

title: Default style bleeds fill to subsequent drawings
description: When drawing multiple rects in sequence with different fill modes (solid then none), the default style system leaks the previous fill color to the next drawing. The defaultStyle.ts module doesn't properly reset when switching between fill types via the Properties panel.

Headed-Chromium dogfood: drew R1 (default fill=none), set fill=#ff00ff via Properties picker, drew R2 (fill=#ff00ff ✓), toggled fill-type to none on R2, drew R3 (fill=none ✓). All transitions clean. title: Pen tool cannot create asymmetric bezier control handles
description: Click-drag on the pen tool creates symmetric incoming/outgoing handles at each anchor point. SVG paths frequently have asymmetric control points (different lengths/angles for incoming vs outgoing handles). Benchmark test showed 44mm error on a control point where the reference had asymmetric handles. Need a modifier key (e.g., Alt+drag) to break handle symmetry and adjust one side independently.

title: Pen tool cannot produce S/s (smooth curveto) path commands
description: The pen tool only generates M, L, C, Z commands. It cannot produce S (smooth curveto) or s (relative smooth curveto), which reflect the previous control point automatically. SVG benchmark paths-data-01 requires S/s for X-curve, Ribbon, and Circle paths. The pen tool would need a mode where clicking an anchor auto-reflects the incoming control handle to create a smooth continuation.

title: yyj-8: font subsetting via mupdf.subsetFonts (optimization)
description: After yyj-4/yyj-7 ship, output PDFs ship the full Carlito (~250KB) regardless of how few glyphs are actually used. Call outDoc.subsetFonts() before save to shrink. title: Expose window.__vfTest hook for headed-test selection
description:
Stories 06 (delete) and 10 (recolor) currently mutate the DOM directly
via page.evaluate because the SelectTool's hit-test only returns
layer-direct children — clicking on a MuPDF-imported text element
selects the wrapping <g>, not the text inside. Without the ability to
drill into wrappers, the PropertiesPanel's fill picker doesn't mount
(detectFillType('none') for the <g>), so h.setFill fails.

Two ways to fix:
  (a) Implement group-drill-in via double-click or a dedicated
      "select content" gesture in selectTool.ts — a UX feature.
  (b) Add a small test-only hook in App.tsx:
        if (import.meta.env.DEV) {
          window.__vfTest = { setSelection, getSelection, history }
        }
      Stories use window.__vfTest.setSelection([textElement]) to
      bypass the click+hit-test layer.

(b) is a 5-line change and unblocks stories 06 / 10 from talking to
the UI properly without committing to a particular drill-in UX. Once
shipped, update those stories to:
  - select via the hook,
  - press Delete (06) / call h.setFill (10),
  - capture export.

(a) is the production-quality fix — separate bead, larger scope.

description: Document the new model. Update API.md: new graftExport.ts surface; live mupdf handle in DocumentState; data-src-* element tagging. Retire obsolete lessons from docs/lessons.md that assumed SVG round-trip as primary (e.g. synthetic-test-blindspot for MuPDF emission shape — still valid for SVG authoring path, but no longer applies to PDF-imported content). Update AGENTS.md 'current state' section. ~1h.

title: [graft/cleanup] Retire pdfExport walk() rendering for PDF-source content
description: Once graftExport is in production and the round-trip byte-diff test is green: delete the source-rendering paths in src/model/pdfExport.ts — drawPath/drawRect/drawLine/drawEllipse/drawCircle/drawImage/drawText. Keep only what pure-SVG export needs (still useful if user authors from scratch). Subset Carlito/Liberation (closes vectorfeld-sqr) now that they're only used for new user-authored text, not full-doc rendering. Likely -300 LOC + -1MB bundle.

title: Subset fonts on export to shrink PDF size
description: Current Export PDF embeds full TTFs (Carlito + Liberation Serif) giving ~1.6MB output for a 1-page flyer. Switching to subset=true broke per-char rendering (vectorfeld-dcx per-char loop + pdf-lib subset machinery interacted badly — scattered char output). Need: find the root cause of the interaction, or restructure so per-char draws emit into a single text run via raw TJ ops so subset works. Target: composite.pdf ≤300KB.

title: Hybrid pdfjs-dist text overlay for Type 3 charproc PDFs
description: Per cd2 research (2026-04-20), pdfjs-dist getTextContent() can extract text from Type 3 charproc fonts that MuPDF renders as paths in the SVG writer. Approach: in pdfRender.worker.ts, also call pdfjs-dist getTextContent() in parallel; for any extracted text item whose bbox doesn't intersect a MuPDF <text>, synthesize a <text> from the pdfjs item AND mark/remove overlapping MuPDF <path>s. Deferred until a real Type 3 fixture is in hand — neither user flyer triggers this case (yellow-BG is outlined-at-creation, recoverable by neither engine; noheader has TrueType subsets that MuPDF preserves). When this lands, vectorfeld can edit text in PDFs that today fall back to paths.

title: Remove unused jspdf + svg2pdf.js from production bundle
description: After vectorfeld-9s9 swapped exportPdf to pdf-lib, jspdf and svg2pdf.js are no longer imported by any src/ file. They still ship in the production bundle because they're listed in package.json dependencies. Action: (1) move jspdf to devDependencies (test fixtures still use it via test/roundtrip/helpers/pdfPipeline.test.ts and renderPdf.test.ts), (2) npm uninstall svg2pdf.js entirely, (3) verify bundle shrinks. Non-urgent — just bundle hygiene.

title: Input focus traps keyboard tool shortcuts
description: When a Properties panel input (SW, X, Y, W, H, Rot, etc.) has focus, pressing a tool shortcut key (r, l, p, e, v) types into the input instead of switching tools. The tool shortcut handler in useToolShortcuts checks for INPUT/TEXTAREA focus, but after pressing Enter to commit a value, focus may remain on the input. Found during benchmark automation: after setSW() the next drawRect's 'r' keypress went into the SW field. Workaround: blur active element before pressing tool keys.

title: Pen tool cannot create multi-subpath elements
description: Each pen tool interaction (Enter to commit) creates a separate <path> element. SVG supports multiple subpaths within a single <path> via the m (relative moveto) command, e.g. 'M 10 10 L 20 20 m 50 0 L 80 80'. The pen tool has no way to start a new subpath within the current path. Benchmark paths-data-01 path 1 (X_curve) uses 'm 0 105' to start a second subpath.

title: Canvas SVG overflows flex container on viewport resize
description: When resizing the browser viewport (e.g. to 1280x1080), the canvas SVG element's natural height (from preserveAspectRatio) exceeds its flex container, causing the html element to scroll 403px. The canvas container has overflow:hidden but the parent flex containers don't have min-h-0 to prevent flex children from expanding. Found via playwright-cli benchmark testing.

title: selectTool moves elements twice per frame with smart guides
description: selectTool.ts:563-579 - elements moved to unsnapped position, then smart guides computed, then moved again. Compute snap algebraically first.
close_reason: Closed

title: Smart guides: split candidates by axis, add early exit
description: smartGuides.ts:108-130 - O(edges*candidates) per frame. Pre-split into x/y arrays, break on bestDist===0.
close_reason: Closed

title: TextPath startOffset change bypasses command history
description: PropertiesPanel.tsx:422-425 - direct DOM mutation, not undoable. Use ModifyAttributeCommand.
close_reason: Closed

title: Pencil tool doesn't auto-switch to select after drawing
description: pencilTool.ts:71-103 - all other drawing tools switch to select+set selection. Pencil doesn't.
close_reason: Closed

title: Duplicated unionBBox and transformedAABB wrappers
description: selection.ts and selectTool.ts both have local unionBBox and transformedAABB wrappers. Consolidate into geometry.ts.
close_reason: Closed

title: Pen tool only emits absolute path commands (no relative c/s/m)
description: The pen tool always generates absolute coordinates (M, L, C, Z). It never produces relative commands (m, l, c, s). While visually equivalent, this means exported SVGs differ structurally from hand-authored or imported SVGs that use relative commands. This is cosmetic — relative vs absolute commands render identically — but noted for completeness in benchmark comparison.

title: PropertiesPanel calls detectFillType 3-4 times per render
description: PropertiesPanel.tsx:469-475 - cache result in local variable instead of calling repeatedly.
close_reason: Closed

value: Rule: when fixing or verifying any user-facing change, dogfood through headed-Chromium playwright end-to-end every time. Unit tests alone are insufficient for visual/layout/PDF bugs. Use temp/composite-via-playwright.mjs pattern or extend it. Never claim done without a headed screenshot.
